verses.text: 'I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.' He feels God's hand turned against him all the day. Deep darkness. The godly are not strangers to seasons of crushing sorrow.
'I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.' He feels God's hand turned against him all the day. Deep darkness. The godly are not strangers to seasons of crushing sorrow.
'I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.' He feels God's hand turned against him all the day. Deep darkness. The godly are not strangers to seasons of crushing sorrow.
God has worn out his flesh, hedged him in, and set him 'in dark places, as they that be dead of old.' Life feels like a grave. Affliction can press the soul into the very shadow of death.
God has worn out his flesh, hedged him in, and set him 'in dark places, as they that be dead of old.' Life feels like a grave. Affliction can press the soul into the very shadow of death.
God has worn out his flesh, hedged him in, and set him 'in dark places, as they that be dead of old.' Life feels like a grave. Affliction can press the soul into the very shadow of death.
He is fenced about so he cannot escape; when he cries, his prayer seems shut out; his paths are walled with hewn stone. Heaven feels closed. Even prayer can seem to go unheard in the darkest hour.
He is fenced about so he cannot escape; when he cries, his prayer seems shut out; his paths are walled with hewn stone. Heaven feels closed. Even prayer can seem to go unheard in the darkest hour.
He is fenced about so he cannot escape; when he cries, his prayer seems shut out; his paths are walled with hewn stone. Heaven feels closed. Even prayer can seem to go unheard in the darkest hour.
God has been to him as a lurking bear and lion, bending His bow and setting him as a target. He feels hunted. The believer may know what it is to feel marked out for suffering.
God has been to him as a lurking bear and lion, bending His bow and setting him as a target. He feels hunted. The believer may know what it is to feel marked out for suffering.
God has been to him as a lurking bear and lion, bending His bow and setting him as a target. He feels hunted. The believer may know what it is to feel marked out for suffering.
The arrows pierce his inmost parts; he is a derision to his people, filled with bitterness and wormwood. Scorned and broken. Affliction is heavier when others mock the sufferer.
The arrows pierce his inmost parts; he is a derision to his people, filled with bitterness and wormwood. Scorned and broken. Affliction is heavier when others mock the sufferer.
The arrows pierce his inmost parts; he is a derision to his people, filled with bitterness and wormwood. Scorned and broken. Affliction is heavier when others mock the sufferer.
His teeth are broken with gravel, his soul covered with ashes, till he says, 'my strength and my hope is perished from the LORD.' He touches the bottom. Yet this confession is the turning point.
His teeth are broken with gravel, his soul covered with ashes, till he says, 'my strength and my hope is perished from the LORD.' He touches the bottom. Yet this confession is the turning point.
His teeth are broken with gravel, his soul covered with ashes, till he says, 'my strength and my hope is perished from the LORD.' He touches the bottom. Yet this confession is the turning point.
Remembering his misery, the wormwood and gall, 'this I recall to mind, therefore have I hope.' He turns a corner. To recall God in the depths is the doorway back to hope.
Remembering his misery, the wormwood and gall, 'this I recall to mind, therefore have I hope.' He turns a corner. To recall God in the depths is the doorway back to hope.
Remembering his misery, the wormwood and gall, 'this I recall to mind, therefore have I hope.' He turns a corner. To recall God in the depths is the doorway back to hope.

'It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed... they are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness. The LORD is my portion.' Here the lament lifts. Even in ruin, mercy survives and morning brings fresh grace.

'It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed... they are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness. The LORD is my portion.' Here the lament lifts. Even in ruin, mercy survives and morning brings fresh grace.

'It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed... they are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness. The LORD is my portion.' Here the lament lifts. Even in ruin, mercy survives and morning brings fresh grace.

'The LORD is good unto them that wait for him'; it is good to hope and quietly wait for His salvation, and good to bear the yoke in youth. Patience is praised. Quiet waiting on God is the wisdom of the afflicted.

'The LORD is good unto them that wait for him'; it is good to hope and quietly wait for His salvation, and good to bear the yoke in youth. Patience is praised. Quiet waiting on God is the wisdom of the afflicted.

'The LORD is good unto them that wait for him'; it is good to hope and quietly wait for His salvation, and good to bear the yoke in youth. Patience is praised. Quiet waiting on God is the wisdom of the afflicted.
Let the sufferer sit alone in silence, put his mouth in the dust, and offer his cheek to the smiter, for there may yet be hope. Humble submission. Bowing low under God's hand is the path to mercy.
Let the sufferer sit alone in silence, put his mouth in the dust, and offer his cheek to the smiter, for there may yet be hope. Humble submission. Bowing low under God's hand is the path to mercy.
Let the sufferer sit alone in silence, put his mouth in the dust, and offer his cheek to the smiter, for there may yet be hope. Humble submission. Bowing low under God's hand is the path to mercy.
'The Lord will not cast off for ever'; though He grieves, He will have compassion, 'for he doth not afflict willingly.' His heart is for us. God's chastening is never the delight of His heart.
'The Lord will not cast off for ever'; though He grieves, He will have compassion, 'for he doth not afflict willingly.' His heart is for us. God's chastening is never the delight of His heart.
'The Lord will not cast off for ever'; though He grieves, He will have compassion, 'for he doth not afflict willingly.' His heart is for us. God's chastening is never the delight of His heart.
To crush prisoners underfoot, to deny a man justice, or to subvert his cause — 'the Lord approveth not.' He hates oppression. The God who chastens His own still loves what is right.
To crush prisoners underfoot, to deny a man justice, or to subvert his cause — 'the Lord approveth not.' He hates oppression. The God who chastens His own still loves what is right.
To crush prisoners underfoot, to deny a man justice, or to subvert his cause — 'the Lord approveth not.' He hates oppression. The God who chastens His own still loves what is right.

Who can make anything happen unless the Lord commands it? 'Wherefore doth a living man complain... for the punishment of his sins?' A sharp question. The living, spared and forgiven, have cause for thanks, not murmuring.

Who can make anything happen unless the Lord commands it? 'Wherefore doth a living man complain... for the punishment of his sins?' A sharp question. The living, spared and forgiven, have cause for thanks, not murmuring.

Who can make anything happen unless the Lord commands it? 'Wherefore doth a living man complain... for the punishment of his sins?' A sharp question. The living, spared and forgiven, have cause for thanks, not murmuring.

'Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the LORD.' We have transgressed, they confess, and Thou hast not pardoned. Self-examination leads home. The way back to God begins with honest searching.

'Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the LORD.' We have transgressed, they confess, and Thou hast not pardoned. Self-examination leads home. The way back to God begins with honest searching.

'Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the LORD.' We have transgressed, they confess, and Thou hast not pardoned. Self-examination leads home. The way back to God begins with honest searching.
They feel God has covered Himself with a cloud so their prayer cannot pass, making them refuse and offscouring among the peoples. Prayer seems blocked. Faith pleads on even when heaven appears shut.
They feel God has covered Himself with a cloud so their prayer cannot pass, making them refuse and offscouring among the peoples. Prayer seems blocked. Faith pleads on even when heaven appears shut.
They feel God has covered Himself with a cloud so their prayer cannot pass, making them refuse and offscouring among the peoples. Prayer seems blocked. Faith pleads on even when heaven appears shut.
All their enemies have opened wide their mouths against them; his eyes run with rivers of tears for his people's destruction. Compassion weeps. A true heart grieves for the ruin of God's people.
All their enemies have opened wide their mouths against them; his eyes run with rivers of tears for his people's destruction. Compassion weeps. A true heart grieves for the ruin of God's people.
All their enemies have opened wide their mouths against them; his eyes run with rivers of tears for his people's destruction. Compassion weeps. A true heart grieves for the ruin of God's people.

His eyes pour down without ceasing, until 'the LORD look down... from heaven.' His weeping wounds his own heart. He will not stop pleading till God regards him. Persistent tears wait for God's looking down.

His eyes pour down without ceasing, until 'the LORD look down... from heaven.' His weeping wounds his own heart. He will not stop pleading till God regards him. Persistent tears wait for God's looking down.

His eyes pour down without ceasing, until 'the LORD look down... from heaven.' His weeping wounds his own heart. He will not stop pleading till God regards him. Persistent tears wait for God's looking down.

Enemies hunted him like a bird, cast him into a pit and a stone upon him, and waters flowed over his head; he said, 'I am cut off.' The end seemed certain. Yet from that very place he would call on God.

Enemies hunted him like a bird, cast him into a pit and a stone upon him, and waters flowed over his head; he said, 'I am cut off.' The end seemed certain. Yet from that very place he would call on God.

Enemies hunted him like a bird, cast him into a pit and a stone upon him, and waters flowed over his head; he said, 'I am cut off.' The end seemed certain. Yet from that very place he would call on God.
'I called upon thy name, O LORD, out of the low dungeon'; God drew near and said, 'Fear not.' Prayer reached heaven. From the deepest pit the cry of faith is heard and answered with peace.
'I called upon thy name, O LORD, out of the low dungeon'; God drew near and said, 'Fear not.' Prayer reached heaven. From the deepest pit the cry of faith is heard and answered with peace.
'I called upon thy name, O LORD, out of the low dungeon'; God drew near and said, 'Fear not.' Prayer reached heaven. From the deepest pit the cry of faith is heard and answered with peace.
'O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.' He asks God to judge his wrongs. The Lord takes up the cause of His own. The Redeemer Himself defends the helpless.
'O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.' He asks God to judge his wrongs. The Lord takes up the cause of His own. The Redeemer Himself defends the helpless.
'O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.' He asks God to judge his wrongs. The Lord takes up the cause of His own. The Redeemer Himself defends the helpless.
He lays before God the enemies' reproach and devices, their sitting down and rising up to mock him. All is known to God. The taunts of the wicked are heard and recorded in heaven.
He lays before God the enemies' reproach and devices, their sitting down and rising up to mock him. All is known to God. The taunts of the wicked are heard and recorded in heaven.
He lays before God the enemies' reproach and devices, their sitting down and rising up to mock him. All is known to God. The taunts of the wicked are heard and recorded in heaven.
He asks God to render them their due, 'according to the work of their hands,' and to remove them from under His heavens. He leaves vengeance to God. Justice is committed wholly into the Lord's hands.
He asks God to render them their due, 'according to the work of their hands,' and to remove them from under His heavens. He leaves vengeance to God. Justice is committed wholly into the Lord's hands.
